configurations.name: with_skill
configurations.notes: All assertions passed. Correct flag usage (-y -p -o text 2>/dev/null + timeout) in all cases. Two evals required retry due to gemini timeout.

configurations.name: without_skill
configurations.notes: 3 assertions failed in code-review: no timeout, no -y flag, stderr not suppressed. Web-search and focused-review passed all assertions.

delta.details: with_skill: 20/20 (100%) vs without_skill: 17/20 (85%). Key gap: operational best practices (timeout, -y, stderr suppression) that baseline agents don't know without the skill.

timing.delta: with_skill +13% slower on average (171s vs 151s), but includes timeout+retry handling that prevented hangs

analyst_observations: The skill's primary value is in operational correctness (timeout, -y, 2>/dev/null) rather than task completion — both configurations successfully invoked gemini and got useful results.
without_skill agents needed an extra step (gemini --help) to discover flags, adding ~5s overhead per eval.
The code-review eval showed the biggest gap: without_skill missed timeout (hang risk), -y flag (tool blocking risk), and stderr suppression (noisy output).
web-search without_skill surprisingly used --yolo correctly after reading --help, but still missed timeout and 2>/dev/null.
The skill's timeout recommendations (60-120s) proved too aggressive for web search — actual web search took >120s requiring retry with 180s timeout.
Non-discriminating assertion: 'gemini CLI invoked' passed in all cases — both configurations can find and run gemini.
Discriminating assertions: 'timeout used', '-y used', 'stderr suppressed' — these only pass with the skill.
